OF | Zebra Technologies | | | ## 1. **OVERVIEW** This work instruction provides instructions on how to create Serial labels using the Zebra system zDSS. ## 2. **CREATE SERIAL LABEL** 1. Log into Zebra- zDSS system. ![](_page_0_Picture_5.jpeg) - 2. Scan the password that is displayed on the monitor (Welcomezebra2). - ✓ The password changes yearly. - 3. Click on zDSS shortcut. ![](_page_0_Picture_9.jpeg) 4. In Scan SMART Login, scan the password also display on the monitor (03testmfg). ![](_page_0_Picture_11.jpeg) ![](_page_0_Picture_12.jpeg) 5. Click on 2D Barcode Maker. ![](_page_0_Picture_14.jpeg) ✓ The Printer IP will auto-populate. #### **CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION WORK INSTRUCTIONS** Zebra Technologies **CREATE AND PRINT SERIAL LABELS Work Instruction No. Revision No. 01 Site Code WI03 Page 2 OF 4** ✓ If it does not auto-populate enter the IP number on the printer. ![](_page_1_Picture_2.jpeg) **Ex**: 6. In the Select Label field drop-down menu, select TX5 Carton. ![](_page_1_Picture_5.jpeg) 7. In the Delivery field, enter a generic delivery number. ![](_page_1_Picture_7.jpeg) - 8. In the Part Number field, enter the SKU number - ✓ Remove any extra digits that auto-populate when the Part # is scanned such as (1P). ![](_page_1_Picture_10.jpeg) ![](_page_1_Picture_11.jpeg) 9. Serial Quantity for 2D barcode field, enter the # of serial(s) that will be on the label. ![](_page_1_Picture_13.jpeg) ✓ Max Serial Quantity is 14. # **CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION WORK INSTRUCTIONS** Zebra Technologies **CREATE AND PRINT SERIAL LABELS Work Instruction No. Revision No. 01 Site Code WI03 Page 3 OF 4** 10. Enter the COO - Country of Origin. ![](_page_2_Figure_2.jpeg) ![](_page_2_Picture_3.jpeg) - ✓ COO abbreviations are in Section 3. - ✓ **Country of Origin cannot be XX.** - 11. In the Select Format field, from the drop-down menu select the format for the serial # you need. ![](_page_2_Picture_7.jpeg) ![](_page_2_Picture_8.jpeg)

Text:
- ✓ A is for the letters on the serial #
- ✓ N is for the numbers on the serial #.
- ✓ The number after the dash is for the # of characters.
- . 12. Click Start

![](_page_2_Picture_13.jpeg)

- ✓ If the part number is scanned instead of the serial # that system will display a Bad Serial # Format error.
- ✓ If the same serial # is scanned the system will not record it and the label will not print.

13. Scan the Serial #(s).

![](_page_2_Picture_18.jpeg)

- 14. The Label Auto prints
- 15. If you have more serial #s of the same format, click Yes at the pop-up.

![](_page_2_Picture_21.jpeg)
